# CLI Checklist: Comfygo Patched Tmux Control

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of CLI tool requirements for patching, launch orchestration, and model enrichment in the Comfygo Patched Tmux Control feature.
**Created**: 2026-06-21
**Feature**: [spec.md](spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It tests the requirements themselves for completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability, and coverage - not the implementation.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are patching requirements defined for the full vendored base (custom_nodes in this repo) plus the two known patches (comfyui and comfy-cli local-nodes)? [Completeness, Spec §US1]
- [ ] CHK002 Is the re-patch after update logic fully specified, including use of versioned patch documentation and historical name/path matching? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements for the full sequential launch (update/patch → verify → tmux launch) defined with up-front settings? [Completeness, Spec §US2, FR-002]
- [ ] CHK004 Are HF git-clone full-data folders and loose file support requirements complete for the enrichment flow? [Completeness, Spec §US3, FR-007]
- [ ] CHK005 Are Civitai query and HF skeleton + side folder + rich JSON requirements fully specified for candidate models? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is 'up-front declarative settings' (storage, keys for patching/launch/enrichment/protection) unambiguously defined (e.g., .comfygo-settings or direnv)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK007 Is tmux window/pane management (name via COMFYGO_LAUNCH_TMUX_NAME, new-window vs attach, pane vs window) clearly specified? [Clarity, Spec §US2, Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK008 Are 'clear actionable message' and 'historical node name/path' for patch mismatch defined with examples? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the 'rich comfygo-model.json' (kind, components, HF + Civitai source info) structure and usage explicitly detailed? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, Key Entities]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK010 Are patching and launch requirements consistent in treating the vendored repo as the source of truth? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012, Constitution I]
- [ ] CHK011 Do enrichment requirements align with existing descriptor/registry machinery without breaking changes? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, FR-013]
- [ ] CHK012 Are privacy requirements (no secrets, keys, private paths in committed docs, issues, specs) consistent across all flows? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011, SC-005]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK013 Are US1 acceptance scenarios measurable (e.g., 'exact set of nodes present and functional', 'self-heals or informs exactly')? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §US1]
- [ ] CHK014 Can the US2 'one command' sequence and control (status, stop) be objectively verified with specific outcomes? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §US2, SC-001]
- [ ] CHK015 Are SC-002 re-patch outcomes (restores using manifests or tells exactly which historical node) testable? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK016 Are requirements defined for the vendored node author changing structure badly (inform with historical name)? [Scenario Coverage, Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK017 Are full flows covered for plain SSH with direnv (single terminal, no X11/GUI)? [Scenario Coverage, Spec §FR-010, SC-004]
- [ ] CHK018 Are re-patch during active tmux session scenarios addressed without disrupting running ComfyUI? [Scenario Coverage, Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK019 Are Civitai/HF API failure scenarios (no token, rate limit, no match) covered with graceful degradation? [Scenario Coverage, Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 Is fallback specified when tmux is not installed on the server? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK021 Are up-front settings file missing or incomplete cases handled with safe defaults and clear user guidance? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK022 Is handling for private data leakage in patch docs, issues, or specs explicitly prohibited? [Edge Case, Spec §SC-005, FR-011]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK023 Are uv-first requirements for all scripts and Python operations explicitly enforced? [Non-Functional, Constitution VI]
- [ ] CHK024 Are existing speckit artifacts (GC harnesses, doctor, verify, descriptor, .comfygo_views) required to remain functional and not removed? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK025 Are privacy and secret safety requirements (no tokens, private paths, usage-revealing details) defined for all committed artifacts? [Non-Functional, Constitution V, Spec §FR-011]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK026 Are assumptions about direnv for tokens and tmux availability documented and validated? [Dependencies, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK027 Are external dependencies (Civitai API, HF) with auth (direnv keys) and graceful failure specified? [Dependencies, Spec §FR-008, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK028 Is preservation of speckit 001/002/003 artifacts explicitly required as non-breaking? [Dependencies, Spec §FR-013]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- Focus: CLI tool requirements quality for patching, launch, enrichment (derived from feature US1/US2/US3)
- Depth: Standard (reviewer/PR)
- This checklist tests whether the requirements are well-written, complete, unambiguous, and ready for implementation.
